name: AWX MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing 49 tools for interacting with AWX (Ansible Tower open source) — the web-based UI and REST API for managing Ansible automation at scale. Enables AI agents to trigger job templates, manage inventories, monitor automation runs, and interact with the full AWX REST API for infrastructure automation workflows.
use_cases: Triggering AWX job templates and workflows from DevOps automation agents
Monitoring Ansible automation job status and logs from orchestration agents
Managing AWX inventories, credentials, and projects from infrastructure agents
Integrating AWX automation into AI-driven incident response and remediation workflows
not_for: Teams not using AWX or Ansible Tower (automation platform-specific)
Direct Ansible playbook execution without AWX (use ansible CLI instead)
Simple script automation without AWX infrastructure in place
best_when: A DevOps team using AWX or Ansible Tower wants AI agents to trigger and monitor infrastructure automation jobs, manage inventories, and integrate Ansible automation into AI-driven workflows.
avoid_when: You don't have AWX deployed. AWX requires a running AWX/Tower instance. For direct Ansible, consider shell-based MCP approaches.

auth.notes: AWX API token or username/password. Configure least-privilege AWX roles for agent access — limit to specific job templates if possible.

pricing.free_tier_limits: AWX is free open source; Ansible Automation Platform (Red Hat) is paid
pricing.paid_tiers: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform for enterprise support
pricing.notes: AWX is free open source (community edition of Ansible Tower). MCP server is free open source from SurgeX-Labs.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by AWX API rate limits and instance capacity

known_agent_gotchas: AWX job templates can trigger real infrastructure changes — scope agent permissions carefully
49 tools is a large surface area — agents may need guidance on which tools to use
AWX API versioning: ensure MCP server targets compatible AWX API version
Self-hosted AWX availability depends on your infrastructure — no SLA from community edition
agent_readiness.security_notes: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Triggers real infrastructure automation. AWX credentials must be secured. Use least-privilege AWX roles. Self-hosted — security depends on your AWX configuration.

security_score_components.security_notes: Infrastructure automation tool. AWX credentials must be secured. Use least-privilege roles. Self-hosted security is operator responsibility.
